events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region
events.description: The war in the Middle East continues, with attacks causing further terror and suffering, deepening the humanitarian crisis across the region. In Geneva, diplomats at the Human Rights Council have been discussing the school strike in Iran’s Minab that killed more than 100 children. Stay with us for live updates on this and from UN agencies providing relief. App users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 27 March: Humanitarian crisis deepens across the region

events.title: World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’
events.description: The latest wave of Russian attacks across Ukraine have reportedly killed and injured civilians and damaged critical infrastructure in Dnipro, Odesa, Poltava, Zaporizhzhia and other regions.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 World News in Brief: Airstrikes continue in Ukraine, refugees return to DR Congo, emergency aid for the ‘Dry Corridor’

events.title: Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on
events.description: President’s declaration allows officials to tackle fuel hoarding or profiteering, while energy secretary says country will lean more heavily on coal Middle East crisis – live updates The Philippines president, Ferdinand Marcos, has declared a state of “national energy emergency” as a result of the Middle East war , which his administration said posed “an imminent danger of a critically low energy supply”. The state of emergency, which will initially last for a year, was declared just hours after the country’s energy secretary said the Philippines planned to boost the output of its coal-fired power plants to keep electricity costs down as the war wreaks havoc with gas shipments. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Philippines declares ‘national energy emergency’ and boosts coal power as Iran war grinds on

events.title: Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears
events.description: Almost one month since Israeli and US airstrikes on Iran began, sparking a wider regional war, UN agencies and partners on Friday highlighted the terror among civilians fleeing bombardment, with “no safe space” to go.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Middle East war: Attacks on vital healthcare, evacuation strike fears

events.title: SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge
events.description: The Security Council is met for an open briefing on Ukraine, requested by European members following Kyiv’s 18 March letter citing a surge in Russian strikes. Inside the country, attacks continue to take a heavy toll: since 19 March, at least 25 civilians have reportedly been killed and more than 130 injured, including children, particularly in Donetsk, Sumy and Zaporizhzhia. We’ll have live coverage below, app users can follow here – and for all our key meetings coverage each day, go here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 SECURITY COUNCIL LIVE: Ukraine in spotlight as Russian strikes surge

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets
events.description: As the war continues to roil the Middle East and compound suffering for civilians across the region, the economic ramifications of the emergency are still playing out, with the Strait of Hormuz the focus of global attention with crude oil prices surging over $100 a barrel again. Meanwhile, settler attacks have escalated dramatically against Palestinians in the occupied West Bank, a topic that we'll be across today also, with aid updates and live reporting from the Security Council. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 24 March: West Bank attacks and Lebanon in focus as Security Council meets

events.title: Cuba says it will do everything to find aid boats missing on way from Mexico
events.description: Two vessels were part of convoy that organisers say was bringing food and medicine to island in face of ‘criminal US blockade’ Cuba’s president, Miguel Díaz-Canel, has said his country will do everything it can to save the people on two missing sailing boats that disappeared while transporting humanitarian aid from Mexico to the Caribbean island. The boats, which set sail from the Mexican state of Quintana Roo last Friday as part of an international aid mission, had been expected to arrive in Havana by Tuesday or Wednesday, the Mexican secretariat of the navy said in a statement. Continue reading...

events.title: Wild autumn weather delivers snow, dangerous swells and power cuts to eastern Australia
events.description: East coast slammed on Thursday night with wind and rain, while Friday brings freezing temperatures and marine warnings Cyclone Narelle to make landfall today and bring possible flash flooding to Perth Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast A dangerous swell has forced the suspension of some ferries across Sydney harbour, the latest in a spate of wild weather that has brought snowfall, power outages and storms to the country’s south-east. Surf Life Saving NSW is warning the highest waves may surge close to 15 metres during the next 24 to 48 hours, which could be the biggest in 100 years, as dangerous swells and strong winds create deadly conditions. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Wild autumn weather delivers snow, dangerous swells and power cuts to eastern Australia

events.title: Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as Russia starts spring offensive
events.description: The Iran war has deflected global attention from Russia’s all-out invasion of its neighbor Ukraine as Europe’s biggest conflict since World War II enters its fifth year and an emboldened Kremlin undertakes a spring offensive
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war deflects attention from Ukraine as Russia starts spring offensive

events.title: Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks
events.description: Pakistan, the only nuclear-armed Muslim state, faces mounting pressure as its Saudi defense pact and Iran ties clash amid the intensifying regional war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran conflict tests Pakistan amid own border clashes as Islamabad touted as venue for US-Tehran talks

events.title: Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns
events.description: The intensifying conflict in the Persian Gulf “has triggered one of the most rapid and severe disruptions to global commodity flows in recent times,” the Chief Economist with the UN Food and Agriculture Organization (FAO) said on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Persian Gulf crisis impacting food security, FAO warns

events.title: Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs
events.description: Mohammad al-Malhi is the seventh Palestinian killed by a settler in a surge of violence since the start of the war with Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Palestinian man killed as death toll from West Bank settler violence climbs

events.title: Iran expert says US ground invasion could cause a global economic meltdown
events.description: Ali Vaez says the US and Iran have reached a stalemate where further escalation would likely lead to catastrophe.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran expert says US ground invasion could cause a global economic meltdown

events.title: Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks
events.description: Latest victims include six people killed in Qom as Iran announces grim milestone of nearly 2,000 war dead.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran emergency workers search for survivors after deadly US-Israel attacks

events.title: Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan
events.description: Market in North Darfur and truck carrying civilians in North Kordofan hit as civil war approaches fourth year At least 28 civilians have been killed in two separate drone strikes in Sudan, according to health workers, as the country’s brutal civil war between the army and the paramilitary Rapid Support Forces (RSF) approaches its fourth year . A strike hit a market in the town of Saraf Omra, in North Darfur state, on Wednesday, killing “22 people, including an infant, and injuring 17 more”, a health worker at the local clinic told Agence France-Presse (AFP). Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Two drone strikes on civilian targets kill 28 people in Sudan

events.title: Europeans to press US Rubio over war in the Middle East at G7 meeting
events.description: US Secretary of State Marco Rubio on Friday arrived at a meeting of G7 foreign ministers in France for talks expected to be dominated by tensions over the war against Iran. Making his first trip abroad since the US-Israeli war on Iran broke out on February 28, Rubio is expected to be pressed by European and other allies over American strategy for the further conduct of the conflict which has led to a spike in global energy prices. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Europeans to press US Rubio over war in the Middle East at G7 meeting

events.title: Burj Khalifa hit by lightning as Dubai suffers flooding after storms and heavy rainfall
events.description: Dubai has been hit by overnight flooding after "intense downpours" hit several cities in the United Arab Emirates (UAE), prompting officials to issue warnings.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Burj Khalifa hit by lightning as Dubai suffers flooding after storms and heavy rainfall

events.title: ‘It’s fired people up’: support grows, including within Labor, for new gas tax to curb wartime profits
events.description: Government, industry and opposition see growing public support for a new gas tax but the industry is fighting back Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The gas industry is mobilising in opposition to a potential new tax on the sector as political momentum builds – including among Labor MPs – for the government to use the May budget to prevent producers profiting from the Middle East war. The Australian Energy Producers (AEP) chief executive, Samantha McCulloch, claimed a new tax would punish the same Asian trading partners Australia was leaning on to supply more fuel amid the global energy crisis. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 ‘It’s fired people up’: support grows, including within Labor, for new gas tax to curb wartime profits

events.title: State Department reveals world's most dangerous countries for Americans
events.description: Americans face risks of "arbitrary arrests" and kidnapping abroad amid State Department warnings for the Middle East, Mexico and Iran-linked threats.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 State Department reveals world's most dangerous countries for Americans

events.title: 'Conflicting and confusing statements from Trump': The latest from Iran
events.description: US President Donald Trump has pushed back his deadline for strikes on Iran's energy assets, saying talks on ending the war were "going very well" as Israel announced fresh strikes on Tehran early Friday. Trump had earlier denied that he was desperate for a deal to end the war, despite the Islamic republic's cool response to an American peace plan. FRANCE 24's Reza Sayah reports from Tehran, Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Conflicting and confusing statements from Trump': The latest from Iran

events.title: World News in Brief: 273 million out of school, deadly attacks on Ukraine, migratory species in danger
events.description: The number of children and young people out of school worldwide has climbed for the seventh consecutive year, reaching 273 million, according to a new report from the UN education agency, UNESCO.

events.title: MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns
events.description: It’s day 26 of war in the Middle East. The UN chief says with the conflict now totally out of control, diplomacy must prevail. Ongoing strikes in Israel and Iran have included intensifying Israeli attacks against Hezbollah targets in Lebanon, while some US troops are heading to the region, the Pentagon has confirmed. Meanwhile, Iran has told the UN maritime agency that the Strait of Hormuz remains open to “non-hostile” ships not associated with the US and Israel. UN News app users can follow coverage here.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 MIDDLE EAST LIVE 25 March: All eyes on Strait of Hormuz; war is ‘out of control’, UN chief warns

events.title: Two humanitarian aid boats heading to Cuba have gone missing, Mexico says
events.description: Navy searching for two boats that left Isla Mujeres last week bound for Havana with nine crew members of different nationalities on board Mexico’s navy said on Thursday it had activated a search-and-rescue operation in the Caribbean to locate two sailboats carrying humanitarian aid to Cuba after the vessels failed to arrive in Havana as scheduled. In a statement, the navy said the two boats left Isla Mujeres, in the Mexican Caribbean state of Quintana Roo, last week bound for Havana with nine crew members of different nationalities on board. Continue reading...

events.title: Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies
events.description: Analysts say the Iran war energy crisis is also adding momentum to nuclear interest and action in the region.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Southeast Asia turns to nuclear as Iran war disrupts energy supplies

events.title: Landslides triggered by heavy rainfall kill at least 20 people in Tanzania
events.description: Heavy rains and strong winds have triggered deadly landslides and floods across East Africa, with southern Tanzania reporting at least 20 deaths
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Landslides triggered by heavy rainfall kill at least 20 people in Tanzania

events.title: US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq reportedly continue as Baghdad warns of 'right to respond'
events.description: Iraq's Prime Minister al-Sudani threatened to confront and respond to U.S. airstrikes on PMF headquarters, calling the attacks violations of Iraqi sovereignty.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US strikes against Iran-backed militias in Iraq reportedly continue as Baghdad warns of 'right to respond'

events.title: Venezuelans deported by US detail fresh claims of torture and abuse at El Salvador mega-prison
events.description: Petition seeks accountability from Salvadorian authorities over human rights violations at notorious Cecot facility A group of 18 Venezuelan men whom the US expelled a notorious Salvadorian mega-prison are demanding that Salvadorian authorities be held internationally accountable for violation of human rights – detailing new allegations of torture, sexual assault and medical neglect. A new petition, filed on Thursday before the Inter-American Commission on Human Rights, alleges that El Salvador violated the human rights of these men, who were expelled to El Salvador’s Terrorism Confinement Center (Cecot) last year without charge. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Venezuelans deported by US detail fresh claims of torture and abuse at El Salvador mega-prison

events.title: Rubio attends G7 meeting in France for talks on Iran, Ukraine
events.description: European foreign ministers on Friday used a G7 meeting with US Secretary of State Marco Rubio to press the case that Russia was helping Iran target US forces in the Middle East war. Rubio joined the second day of the gathering of ministers of leading Western democracies, taking place amid wars ​in Iran and Ukraine, ‌economic uncertainty and mounting unease over unpredictable US foreign policy under Donald Trump. FRANCE 24's International Affairs Commentator Douglas Herbert tells us more.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Rubio attends G7 meeting in France for talks on Iran, Ukraine

events.title: UN chief calls for immediate release of 118 detained UN staff
events.description: UN Secretary-General António Guterres has called for the immediate release of the 118 UN staff currently detained worldwide as attacks against the United Nations increase with 179 personnel arrested or detained last year alone.

events.title: Nigeria takes its place on world stage in quest to become regional superpower
events.description: Nigeria and UK look to strengthen trade and economic ties amid growing calls from Africa and Caribbean for reparative justice “There are chapters in our shared history that I know have left some painful marks,” King Charles said during a state banquet to welcome the Nigerian president, Bola Tinubu, to the UK, in a year in which the monarch is expected to come under renewed pressure to make a formal apology for transatlantic slavery and colonialism. But while demands grow from African and Caribbean nations for the UK to further reparative justice, Nigeria and the UK are looking to the future of global trade. Continue reading...

events.title: WA premier issues transparency warning to fuel suppliers – as it happened
events.description: This blog is now closed Pauline Hanson’s One Nation likely to win second South Australian seat Albanese government says fuel supply ‘same, if not higher’ in coming weeks as Coalition calls for halving of excise Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Power outages in Sydney after storms hit city overnight Power outages continue across Sydney this morning after heavy storms battered the city and surrounding areas overnight. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ WA premier issues transparency warning to fuel suppliers – as it happened

events.title: UN calls for solidarity and political will to stamp out racial discrimination
events.description: Nearly 70 years after South African police fired upon peaceful demonstrators in Sharpeville protesting apartheid-era laws, killing 69, the UN renewed the commitment to work for justice and equality on Monday, marking the International Day for the Elimination of Racial Discrimination.

events.title: India: When floods come, survival is a roll of the dice
events.description: Across India's flood-prone regions, marginalized communities are often hit the hardest by escalating climate-related disasters.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ India: When floods come, survival is a roll of the dice

events.title: France slams return of Olympic gender testing as 'a step backwards'
events.description: The International Olympic Committee's decision to reintroduce gender testing for the first time since 1999 is a "step backwards", French Sports Minister Marina Ferrari said on Friday. She said that genetic testing would unfairly rule out many athletes with differences in sexual development – previously known as intersex athletes.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 France slams return of Olympic gender testing as 'a step backwards'

events.title: Opus Dei: Spain under influence of ultra-conservative Catholic movement
events.description: The ultra-conservative Catholic organisation Opus Dei was founded a century ago in Spain. Today, it's an international movement with around 90,000 members. In its home country, it has managed to infiltrate all spheres of modern society, from schools to hospitals. Despite serious allegations against it, Opus Dei remains a powerful force in Spain. FRANCE 24's Maude Petit-Jové, Mathilde Lopinski, Emile Roger and Sarah Morris report.

events.title: Video: Children’s toys found in rubble of US-Israeli strike in Iran
events.description: Rescue teams from the IRCS have been searching the rubble of residential buildings hit in US-Israeli attacks on Iran.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Video: Children’s toys found in rubble of US-Israeli strike in Iran

events.title: German deepfake porn case sparks protests and pressure for change in law
events.description: Germany's government is facing pressure to toughen laws against digital violence after a prominent television actor accused her former husband of posting AI-generated porn resembling her on fake online accounts purporting to belong to her. The case has sparked a national conversation on new forms of violence against women in the online sphere and heaped pressure on Chancellor Friedrich Merz's government to ​close legal loopholes. FRANCE 24's Nick Holdsworth reports from Berlin, Germany.
events.ticker_label: 💥 German deepfake porn case sparks protests and pressure for change in law

events.title: Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians
events.description: The death toll from a horrific attack on a hospital in Sudan’s Darfur has risen further, amid a “sharp increase” in drone attacks against civilians this year, UN agencies said on Tuesday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Sudan: Hospital strike highlights surge in drone attacks on civilians

events.title: Helicopter crashes into ocean off Hawaii coast, leaving multiple dead and injured
events.description: Three people were killed and two others injured Thursday when a helicopter operated by Airborne Aviation crashed into the ocean off Kauai, Hawaii.
events.ticker_label: ✈️ Helicopter crashes into ocean off Hawaii coast, leaving multiple dead and injured

events.title: US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns
events.description: The 82nd Airborne Division deployment to the Middle East is intended to pressure Iran into accepting U.S. ceasefire terms, military analyst Michael Eisenstadt says.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 US troops brace for ‘hit-and-run’ guerilla attacks as 82nd Airborne deploys to Iran, military analyst warns

events.title: Marco Rubio joins G7 talks with focus on Iran war
events.description: It is the secretary of state's first foreign trip since the conflict began last month.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Marco Rubio joins G7 talks with focus on Iran war

events.title: Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns
events.description: Malcolm Turnbull asks defence department official what Australia would do if the promised Virginia-class and Aukus-class submarines don’t arrive Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons the Aukus deal with the US and UK, a senior defence official has warned, declining to publicly countenance an alternative plan if Australia’s promised nuclear-powered fleet does not arrive under Australian command. “Defence has been directed to pursue Aukus and we are pursuing Aukus and that’s our plan. I would not venture into the space about ‘Plan B’ or ‘Plan C’,” defence department deputy secretary, Hugh Jeffrey, told a Sovereignty and Security Forum in Canberra on Friday. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Australia will be left with no submarines if it abandons Aukus, senior defence official warns

events.title: 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes

events.ticker_label: 🪖 'Americas Counter Cartel Coalition': Inside the US strategy to combat narco terror, confront China, other foes

events.title: UK counterterrorism police probe antisemitic arson attack as Iran-linked group claims responsibility
events.description: An Iran-linked group claims responsibility for torching Jewish ambulances outside a Golders Green synagogue in London as counterterrorism police investigate.

events.title: India lowers fuel taxes, says rumours of lockdown ‘completely false’
events.description: India, the world's third biggest crude importer, announces measures amid shortages created by the Iran war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 India lowers fuel taxes, says rumours of lockdown ‘completely false’

events.title: Nicolás Maduro appears again in New York court on ‘narco-terrorism’ charges
events.description: Deposed Venezuelan president and his wife, who both pleaded not guilty, were captured by US military in January The deposed Venezuelan president Nicolás Maduro appeared in a Manhattan federal court on Thursday for his “narco-terrorism” case after his capture by US military forces earlier this year. The hearing opened with the defense and prosecution arguing over whether Maduro should be allowed to use Venezuelan government funds to pay for his defense. The defense has insisted that the US is violating the deposed leader’s constitutional rights by blocking government money from being used for his legal costs. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 💥 Nicolás Maduro appears again in New York court on ‘narco-terrorism’ charges

events.title: Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump
events.description: Iran has been sending not only missiles around the region but also trolling tweets and videos around the internet. It's the latest in global diplomacy.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran's war propaganda includes memes directed at Trump

events.title: Exclusive images: Nightly pro-govt rallies spread in Iran
events.description: Since the war between the US, Israel and Iran began on February 28th, an increasing number of Iranians have been gathering in Tehran's main squares, but also in areas across the country, to express their support of the regime. With exclusive images, some of these Iranians tell us why they make the nightly trip.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Exclusive images: Nightly pro-govt rallies spread in Iran

events.title: World News in Brief, Cuba blockade hits healthcare, Haiti’s ‘vortex of violence’, deadly Djibouti shipwreck
events.description: Hospitals are just the latest vital public services to be hit in Cuba, more than a month since Washington took measures to block oil supplies from entering the Caribbean nation.

events.title: Fox News ‘Antisemitism Exposed’ Newsletter: Harvard 'indifferent to hostility' toward Jewish students: lawsuit
events.description: Fox News' "Antisemitism Exposed" newsletter brings you stories on the rising anti-Jewish prejudice across the U.S. and the world.

events.title: NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive
events.description: Russia launched nearly 400 drones and missiles at Ukraine, prompting Poland and Romania to scramble NATO fighter jets as strikes neared their airspace.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 NATO scrambles jets as Russia fires nearly 400 drones toward Ukraine, signaling new spring offensive

events.title: Two Cuba-bound aid ships missing after leaving Mexico
events.description: Mexico's navy launches a rescue mission for ships delivering aid to Cuba, strained by food and fuel shortages amid a US embargo.

events.title: How Russia's threat has seen Germany become Europe's most important army
events.description: Germany once terrorised Europe, but now Europe is welcoming a resurgent German military.

events.title: War on Iran price crisis not just about energy, says analyst
events.description: Abdulla Banndar Al-Etaibi, says the disruption of fertiliser supplies due to the war on Iran will push food prices up.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 War on Iran price crisis not just about energy, says analyst

events.title: Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war
events.description: US president says he will host Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit later this year Middle East crisis – live updates Donald Trump will meet Xi Jinping in May during the US president’s first visit to China in eight years, a closely watched trip that had been postponed due to the Iran war. Trump was initially slated to travel next week, but will now visit Beijing on 14 and 15 May, he wrote in a post on Truth Social on Wednesday. Trump said he would host the Chinese leader in a reciprocal visit in Washington later this year. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Trump’s trip to meet Xi Jinping in China rescheduled for May due to Iran war

events.title: Weather tracker: Cold weather sweeps Europe and cyclone hits Australia
events.description: Low pressure brings unsettled conditions to southern Europe, and rain and snow to western and central areas Southern Europe has been under a variety of severe weather warnings this week owing to widely unsettled conditions driven by an area of low pressure in the region. This area of low pressure – previously part of the system that brought colder conditions to swathes of the UK earlier this week – moved southwards across Europe through the middle of the week. In doing so, it brought a cold front across western and central parts of Europe, with spells of rain and hill snow across the Alps on Wednesday, followed by snow showers on a brisk north-westerly wind. By Friday morning, accumulations of 20-40cm were expected above 600 metres, and 60-100cm above 1,000 metres in the Swiss Alps. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Weather tracker: Cold weather sweeps Europe and cyclone hits Australia

events.title: Iran war: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?
events.description: Supply disruptions, high prices, nervous markets — the war in Iran brings back memories of the oil crises of the 1970s. Back then, many economies plunged into stagflation. How bad is the current crisis?
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war: Is another 1970s‑style oil crisis looming?

events.title: Two aid boats go missing en route to Cuba, says Mexico
events.description: Two humanitarian aid boats with nine people onboard have not reached Havana after setting sail from Mexico. The Mexican navy is now conducting a search and rescue operation.

events.title: G7 allies press Rubio on US Iran plans, discuss Strait of Hormuz
events.description: G7 allies were on Friday pressing US Secretary of State Marco Rubio for clarity on American plans for Iran almost one month into the war in the Middle East, with concern also intensifying over the extent of cooperation between Russia and the Islamic republic. Before leaving for France, Rubio said Thursday that it was in the "interest" of all G7 nations to push for the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz. FRANCE 24's Antonia Kerrigan reports.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 G7 allies press Rubio on US Iran plans, discuss Strait of Hormuz

events.title: Pakistan the peacemaker? Islamabad steps up as US-Iran mediator
events.description: On this week's Access Asia we look at how Pakistan has emerged as a key mediator to end the war in Iran. The Atlantic Council's Michael Kugelman tells us the conflict spillover risks are particularly acute, adding that Pakistan badly wants to avoid getting dragged into the war.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Pakistan the peacemaker? Islamabad steps up as US-Iran mediator

events.title: Iran war tests MAGA's 'America first' credo
events.description: Donald #Trump was elected on the promise to put 'America first', ruling out foreign wars. As the conflict in #Iran nears its first month, support among #MAGA remains high - but some cracks are appearing.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Iran war tests MAGA's 'America first' credo

events.title: UK told to prepare for 'worst case' without US defence
events.description: The UK and its NATO allies in Europe must prepare for a "worst case scenario" of the US not defending them in a crisis, an influential committee of peers and MPs has warned.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 UK told to prepare for 'worst case' without US defence

events.title: Why Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran
events.description: Pakistan has emerged as an unexpected mediator, offering to help bring Washington and Tehran to the negotiating table as fears of a wider regional conflict escalate following U.S. and Israeli strikes on Iran that began in late February
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Why Pakistan has emerged as a mediator between US and Iran

events.title: Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office
events.description: More than four years since Russia’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine, the “danger is only increasing”, particularly from attack drones, a top UN human rights official warned on Thursday.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Ukraine: danger is only increasing, warns UN human rights office

events.title: They bought an abandoned 'ghost house' in the Japanese countryside
events.description: He'd spent years backpacking around the world, and Japanese traveler Daisuke Kajiyama was finally ready to return home to pursue his long-held dream of opening up a guesthouse.

events.title: The Olympic committee bans trans athletes from women's events, raising many questions
events.description: The International Olympic Committee will require all athletes who want to participate in women's events to undergo genetic testing. The policy takes effect for the 2028 Summer Games in Los Angeles.

events.title: Merz criticized for selective approach to international law
events.description: Dozens of legal scholars have called on the German government to condemn the US operations in Iran and Venezuela. They argue that if violations of international law go unpunished, the rule of law erodes.

events.title: Donald Trump takes credit for transgender ban at LA's 2028 Olympics
events.description: PRESS REVIEW – Friday, March 27: We look at reactions in the Indian press after parliament approved a controversial law which critics say will curtail the rights of transgender people. The International Olympic Committee makes a landmark ruling to forbid transgender female athletes from participating in female events, beginning with LA's 2028 Olympics. A bandaged Lindsey Vonn graces the cover of Vanity Fair and talks about her Winter Olympics ordeal. Plus: Japan's viral Punch the monkey has found love!

events.title: Effort to create buffer zone in Lebanon positions Israel as 'aggressive force requiring resistance'
events.description: Alison Sergent is pleased to welcome Dr Filippo Dionigi, Senior Lecturer in Politics and International Relations at the University of Bristol. According to Dr Dionigi, the expansion of Israeli control under the rationale of a “buffer zone” reflects a recurring strategic logic that has been attempted before, notably between 1982 and 2000. Yet history suggests that such measures often produce unintended consequences. Hezbollah’s legitimacy, especially within the Shia community, is not only military but narrative-driven, grounded in claims of resistance.

events.title: Gaza: Commitment to US-backed plan crucial to recovery, Security Council hears
events.description: As tensions escalate in the Middle East, the international community must not lose sight of the situation in Gaza, an official with US President Donald Trump’s Board of Peace across the shattered enclave said on Tuesday in his first appearance in the UN Security Council.

events.title: Putin 'asks oligarchs to donate to budget' as cost of Ukraine war soars
events.description: Vladimir Putin has asked oligarchs to donate to Russia's budget to stabilise the country's finances as he presses on with the war in Ukraine, according to Russian media.
events.ticker_label: 🪖 Putin 'asks oligarchs to donate to budget' as cost of Ukraine war soars

events.title: US military sends drones, alongside 200 troops, to Nigeria amid fears of renewed Boko Haram insurgency
events.description: The U.S. military deployed MQ-9 Reaper drones to Nigeria for intelligence-gathering and training as the African country is grappling with a security crisis.

events.title: North Korean dictator says government will keep cementing nation's 'irreversible status as a nuclear power'

events.title: UN rights chief urges US to conclude probe into deadly Iran school attack

events.title: UN’s landmark slavery ruling energises African Union’s fight for reparations
events.description: Despite resistance from states who had role in chattel slavery, many feel this is an idea whose time has come John Mahama knows a thing or two about beating the establishment. On Wednesday, less than two years after completing a remarkable comeback as Ghana’s president with a landslide defeat of the ruling party candidate, he rallied the world to ratify a landmark vote against transatlantic chattel slavery, despite major opposition from the same western entities that drove it for centuries. The resolution to declare the practice as “the gravest crime against humanity” passed with a decisive majority at the UN general assembly and has been largely welcomed across Africa. Yet the details of the tally reveal a world still deeply divided on the gravity of the sin of enslaving more than 15 million people as chattel over the course of 400 years. Continue reading...
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ UN’s landmark slavery ruling energises African Union’s fight for reparations

events.title: ‘They can reach me wherever’: China using financial tactics to coerce people who flee, says report
events.description: UK urged to tackle transnational repression, as dissidents say Beijing has targeted them with tax bills and other threats “I didn’t feel safe, even though I’m not based in Hong Kong any more,” said Christopher Mung Siu-tat after getting tax bills from Hong Kong authorities. “The regime can reach me by their long arms wherever I am.” Siu-tat, the executive director at the Hong Kong Labour Rights Monitor, a UK-based NGO, fled Beijing’s sweeping national security laws years ago. The letters are the latest example of a series of transnational repression (TNR) tactics the 54-year-old has faced in recent years. Continue reading...

events.title: Israel says Iranian leader who ordered Strait of Hormuz closure killed in targeted strike

This ​decision was communicated to Pretoria by ​the French embassy ​about two ​weeks ago, said ​Vincent Magwenya, a spokesperson for South African President ⁠Cyril Ramaphosa. FRANCE 24's Eunice Masson reports from Cape Town, South Africa.

events.title: Older Australians in ‘immediate danger’ as fuel crisis affects travel for aged care workers

Reports of small mining businesses scaling back operations and some construction companies deciding against hiring more apprentices have prompted suggestions the government should step in to help, with the Master Builders Association already forecasting a downturn in the number of homes that will be built this year. events.title: Nepal: Ex-rapper Balendra Shah sworn in as prime minister

events.title: #MeToo and the French farming sector
events.description: In this edition, we meet French farmer Laura Chalendard, who reported a sexual assault in 2023 and when no charges followed, launched a #MeToo movement in the agricultural sector to empower women to speak out against sexual violence. Also how perimenopause and menopause in female employees are worsening Germany's labour shortage and prompting a number of companies to confront what has long been one of the last workplace taboos. Plus how a team of elderly women in eastern Uganda use cricket to ease stress and field loneliness while staying on the front foot against health challenges.

Maduro cut a relaxed figure on Thursday as he made his second court appearance since his capture by US forces in an extraordinary nighttime raid. FRANCE 24's Jessica Le Masurier reports from New York City.

events.title: ‘The violence of racist tyranny’: African Guernica goes on display alongside Picasso masterpiece
events.description: Piece by late South African artist Dumile Feni is part of new series History Doesn’t Repeat Itself, But It Does Rhyme On the second floor of the Reina Sofía, in the very spot where Picasso’s Guernica was first exhibited when it arrived in the Madrid museum 34 years ago, there now hangs a smaller, near-namesake of the Spanish artist’s most famous work. While African Guernica, which was drawn by the late South African artist Dumile Feni in 1967, may lack the scale of Picasso’s masterpiece, its depth, anger and unnerving juxtaposition of man and beast, light and dark, and innocence and cruelty, are every bit as disturbing. Continue reading...

events.title: Flatterers out in force to fill Trump’s head with Venezuelan statue dreams
events.description: President’s popularity top of mind at another weird and wild cabinet meeting – riff on merits of Sharpies included They have become so notorious for displays of flattery and obsequiousness that critics have drawn comparisons with North Korea. Thursday’s cabinet meeting at the White House was no different. Doug Burgum , the US interior secretary, outflanked his fellow praise singers by saying he believes that Venezuela – which the US attacked in January – intends to honour the president with a statue. Continue reading...

events.title: Australia refuses to say how many Chinese nationals are arriving by boat, saying it may damage bilateral relations
events.description: Exclusive: Indonesia reports growing number of attempts by Chinese nationals to organise boat journeys, as Australian authorities refuse to reveal details Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast The Australian government has refused to reveal how many Chinese nationals have arrived in Australia by boat since 2024, saying that disclosing the figure may harm relations with other countries. However, reports by Indonesian police show that there has been a consistent trend of Chinese nationals attempting to reach Australia through Indonesia as an alternative to “zouxian”, or “walking the line” – the illegal migration route through Central America to the US via the Darién Gap between Colombia and Panama. Continue reading...

events.title: Silicon Valley Bank collapse renews calls to address disparities impacting entrepreneurs of color

events.title: Spanish woman, 25, dies by legal euthanasia in case that drew national spotlight
events.description: For nearly two years, Castillo pursued her right to die after her father put up a lengthy legal battle when a medical body in Catalonia approved her request for euthanasia in 2024. As the family's struggle unfolded, Castillo’s case was closely followed in Spain, which passed legislation in 2021 enshrining the right to euthanasia and medically assisted suicide for patients meeting certain conditions. Castillo's young age, the public battle waged by her family to stop her and the circumstances that led her to seek euthanasia animated public opinion as the courts ultimately ruled in favor of her right to end her life.

events.title: Slovenia says it has confirmed foreign influence on last weekend's election

events.title: Norway to add more than $11bn to defence budget over 10 years
events.description: Norway, as well as other NATO countries, has been under pressure from the US to boost defence spending.

events.title: Saudi, UAE, Iraq: Can three pipelines help oil escape Strait of Hormuz?
events.description: Countries in the Middle East have ramped up oil exports via pipeline to bridge the Strait of Hormuz gap.

events.title: Nepal’s new PM gives message to nation in rap, ahead of swearing in
events.description: Nepali rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah has been sworn in as PM after his party’s landslide election win.
events.ticker_label: 🌪️ Nepal’s new PM gives message to nation in rap, ahead of swearing in

events.title: ‘We will no longer stand by’: Austria plans social media ban for under-14s
events.description: Austrian officials highlight addiction and ill-health while advocating for stricter age restrictions.

events.title: Nepal’s youngest premier sworn in after releasing new rap song about unity
events.description: At 35, Balen Shah becomes Nepal's first Madhesi prime minister, representing the southern plains bordering India.

events.title: Markets digest bank earnings after recent turmoil

events.title: Still haven't filed your taxes? Here's what you need to know
events.description: So far this tax season, the IRS has received more than 90 million income tax returns for 2022.

events.title: Analysis: Fox News is about to enter the true No Spin Zone
events.description: This is it.

events.title: Scientists identify secret ingredient in Leonardo da Vinci paintings
events.description: "Old Masters" such as Leonardo da Vinci, Sandro Botticelli and Rembrandt may have used proteins, especially egg yolk, in their oil paintings, according to a new study.

events.title: 'A definitive backslide.' Inside fashion's worrying runway trend
events.description: Now that the Fall-Winter 2023 catwalks have been disassembled, it's clear one trend was more pervasive than any collective penchant for ruffles, pleated skirts or tailored coats.

events.title: Michael Jordan's 1998 NBA Finals sneakers sell for a record $2.2 million
events.description: In 1998, Michael Jordan laced up a pair of his iconic black and red Air Jordan 13s to bring home a Bulls victory during Game 2 of his final NBA championship — and now they are the most expensive sneakers ever to sell at auction. The game-winning sneakers sold for $2.2 million at Sotheby's in New York on Tuesday, smashing the sneaker auction record of $1.47 million, set in 2021 by a pair of Nike Air Ships that Jordan wore earlier in his career.

events.title: Here's the real reason to turn on airplane mode when you fly
events.description: We all know the routine by heart: "Please ensure your seats are in the upright position, tray tables stowed, window shades are up, laptops are stored in the overhead bins and electronic devices are set to flight mode."

events.title: 'I was up to my waist down a hippo's throat.' He survived, and here's his advice
events.description: Paul Templer was living his best life.

events.title: Relaxed entry rules make it easier than ever to visit this stunning Asian nation
events.description: Due to its remoteness and short summer season, Mongolia has long been a destination overlooked by travelers.

events.title: The most beautiful sections of China's Great Wall
events.description: Having lived in Beijing for almost 12 years, I've had plenty of time to travel widely in China.

events.title: Sign up to our newsletter for a weekly roundup of travel news

events.title: Senate votes to fund most of DHS. And, Trump extends Iran's deadline to reopen strait
events.description: The Senate has voted to fund most of the Department of Homeland Security. And, President Trump extends the deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

events.title: Cuban Americans face scrutiny for sending goods back to the island
events.description: Cuban Americans who ship goods to relatives on the island are now seen as propping up Cuba's communist regime as the economy there continues to deteriorate.

events.title: The U.S. and Iran can't even agree on whether they're talking
events.description: President Trump says Iran is "begging" for a deal. Iran says it has no intention of negotiating. NPR's Mary Louise Kelly talks with NPR's Tom Bowman and Aya Batrawy about prospects for an off-ramp.

events.title: Germany: Mental health patients struggle to get help
events.description: Waiting lists for psychotherapy are full-to-bursting in Germany with patients often waiting over a year for treatment. Now a row has irrupted over planned fee cuts for therapists in the public healthcare system.

events.title: Nepal's new PM inherits India-China-US balancing act
events.description: Rapper-turned-politician Balendra Shah is leading Nepal's government, taking office with a youth-led mandate for change. But how will the political newcomer deal with Nepal's tricky geopolitics?

events.title: US: Senate ends shutdown with ICE-free DHS funding bill
events.description: The US Senate has approved funds for TSA and most of Homeland Security but not ICE, after a partial government shutdown triggered massive disruption at airports, and 50,000 TSA agents were forced to work without pay.

events.title: EU to target Temu, Shein and other companies with a levy on small packages coming from outside the bloc
events.description: Temu, Shein and other companies are set to be affected by handling fees for non-EU small packages from November 1. The fee will be charged in addition to a customs tax on such items that goes into effect in July.

events.title: Whale swims to freedom after days stranded on German beach
events.description: The humpback whale was first spotted stuck near Timmendorfer Strand off Germany's Baltic coast on Monday.

events.title: In a first, Trump signature to appear on all US dollar bills
events.description: The signature on US currency is just the latest instance of the president’s name and likeness being stamped onto government projects.

events.title: US Judge declines to dismiss Maduro case over legal fees
events.description: Nicolas Maduro asked the judge to dismiss the case by arguing he did not have sufficient personal funds for his legal defense. The Trump administration has barred the Venezuelan government from paying the fees.

events.title: Gang-rape victim, 25, dies by euthanasia after father's legal challenge fails
events.description: A paraplegic gang-rape victim has died by euthanasia after her father's failed legal challenge.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Gang-rape victim, 25, dies by euthanasia after father's legal challenge fails

events.title: Nepal's new prime minister chooses auspicious time to take oath of office
events.description: Nepal’s youngest prime minister will take the oath of office in a ceremony shaped as much by astrology as politics

events.title: Nepal's newly elected parliament is sworn in months after a youth-led revolt
events.description: Nepal’s newly elected members of parliament have been sworn in, with nearly two-thirds of them from a political party that is less than four years old

events.title: Italy seizes millions in assets allegedly stolen from Bond co-star Ursula Andress
events.description: Italian police say they have seized about 20 million euros in property and assets linked to money stolen from Swiss actress and original Bond girl Ursula Andress

events.title: What to know as Africans welcome UN vote on slavery reparations
events.description: The U.N. General Assembly’s resolution declaring the trafficking of enslaved Africans “the gravest crime against humanity” and calling for reparations is being welcomed across Africa

events.title: EU lawmakers vote to make it easier to set up migrant detention centers outside the bloc
events.description: European lawmakers have approved a plan that makes it easier to send rejected asylum seekers to new detention centers outside the EU

events.title: Police in Kenya exhume at least 33 bodies from a mass grave
events.description: Authorities in western Kenya say they have uncovered a mass grave with at least 33 bodies

events.title: Bus sinks in Bangladesh river, killing at least 18 people
events.description: Officials in Bangladesh say a bus carrying about 50 people has plunged into a river, killing at least 18

events.title: Pope Leo XIV makes a rare 1-day visit to glitzy Monaco
events.description: Pope Leo XIV is going to the principality of Monaco for a one-day trip

events.title: Haitian gangs expand reach as police are accused of ‘summary executions’
events.description: Deadly gangs in Haiti are expanding their reach to include control over key sea and road routes as police in the beleaguered Caribbean island nation are being accused of using “unnecessary and disproportionate lethal force and summary executions.”

events.title: Senate passes overnight bill to fund most of Homeland Security as fight nears end and more top headlines
events.description: Get all the stories you need-to-know from the most powerful name in news delivered first thing every morning to your inbox.

events.title: New Jersey middle school teacher charged with child sexual assault after alleged relationship with student
events.description: A former New Jersey middle school teacher faces multiple charges after allegedly engaging in a sexual relationship with a student in 2021.

events.title: United jet dodges Black Hawk in last-second maneuver over California airport: 'That was not good'
events.description: The FAA is investigating a close call between a United Airlines Boeing 737 and a Black Hawk helicopter at John Wayne Airport in Santa Ana, California.

events.title: DHS arrests 5 illegal immigrants convicted of violent crimes including manslaughter, child assault
events.description: ICE arrested five illegal immigrants convicted of manslaughter, child sexual assault, and carjacking, according to DHS Acting Assistant Secretary Lauren Bis.

events.title: Decorated Detroit police sergeant led ‘double life’ as serial rapist in disturbing case: prosecutor
events.description: Retired Detroit police Sgt. Benjamin Wagner, 68, was charged with 14 counts, including kidnapping and serial rape, and is accused of living a secret double life.

events.title: Veteran, 83, allegedly shoved onto NYC subway tracks dies from injuries; illegal migrant charged with murder
events.description: Richard Williams, an 83-year-old retired Air Force veteran, died after being randomly shoved onto NYC subway tracks by an illegal immigrant, DHS says.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Veteran, 83, allegedly shoved onto NYC subway tracks dies from injuries; illegal migrant charged with murder

events.title: Driver plows through airport gate onto airfield, sneaks onto planes before police takedown, bodycam shows
events.description: Bryan Parker, 58, allegedly drove drunk onto the Daytona Beach airport airfield and tried boarding planes. He faces aircraft piracy and DUI charges.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Driver plows through airport gate onto airfield, sneaks onto planes before police takedown, bodycam shows

events.title: Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies
events.description: Zelenskyy claimed U.S. security guarantees for Ukraine are tied to ceding Donbas to Russia, but the White House says those claims are false.
events.ticker_label: 💥 Zelenskyy claims US tied Ukraine security guarantees to giving up Donbas, White House denies

events.title: EU blocks US vote to define gender as biological men and women at UN women’s forum
events.description: The EU used a procedural motion to block a U.S. resolution at the United Nations that sought to define gender as referring to biological men and women, leaving it without a vote.
